title: Esme and the Magic Library Books
synopsis: When Esme discovers that the fairy tale books in her school library have come to life, she must help the mixed-up characters find their way back to the right stories before the library closes.

pages.text: Esme loved library time at school. She had dark curly hair and bright brown eyes that sparkled when she read. Today, she walked into the library with her class. Mrs. Chen, their teacher, smiled at them. "Choose any book you want," she said. Esme's friend Marcus pointed to the fairy tale section. "Let's look there!" he said. Aisha nodded. She loved princesses. The three friends walked to the tall bookshelf.

illustration.description: A diverse group of children entering a bright, colorful school library. Esme, a girl with dark curly hair and brown eyes, walks with Marcus (a boy with medium skin tone and short black hair) and Aisha (a girl wearing a colorful hijab). Mrs. Chen, an Asian teacher with kind eyes and a warm smile, gestures welcomingly. The library has tall bookshelves filled with colorful books.
illustration.alt: Esme and her friends entering the school library with their teacher

pages.text: Something strange was happening. The books on the fairy tale shelf were glowing! "Do you see that?" whispered Esme. Marcus rubbed his eyes. "The books are shiny," he said. Aisha giggled. "Maybe they're magic!" Just then, a tiny voice squeaked, "Help! Help!" The voice came from a red book. Esme looked around. Mrs. Chen was helping other kids. No one else seemed to notice. "Should we open it?" asked Marcus.

illustration.description: The three children staring in wonder at a bookshelf where fairy tale books are glowing with soft, magical light. Esme points at a red book that's glowing brightest. The children's faces show excitement and curiosity with wide, expressive eyes. The library background is slightly dimmed to emphasize the magical glow from the books.
illustration.alt: Children discovering glowing fairy tale books on the library shelf

pages.text: Esme carefully opened the red book. POOF! Out popped a tiny girl in a red hood. But she was green! "I'm Little Red Riding Hood," she said. "But I'm supposed to be normal colored, not green!" Behind her came a small wolf. The wolf was bright pink! "And I'm supposed to be gray!" huffed the wolf. "We got mixed up with another story!" More tiny characters tumbled out. A purple Goldilocks! A blue Cinderella! They were all the wrong colors!

illustration.description: A magical scene showing tiny fairy tale characters emerging from an open red book in a puff of sparkly smoke. Little Red Riding Hood is green, the wolf is bright pink, Goldilocks is purple, and Cinderella is blue. The children watch in amazement as the colorful characters appear, floating slightly above the book with surprised expressions.
illustration.alt: Tiny, colorfully mixed-up fairy tale characters popping out of an open book

pages.text: "We need to find our right stories!" cried the green Little Red Riding Hood. "Or we'll be stuck like this forever!" The pink wolf nodded sadly. "I look silly," he said. Esme thought hard. "Maybe you got mixed up with other books," she said. "Let's look for more glowing books!" Marcus spotted a green book glowing nearby. "Look!" he pointed. Aisha found a purple one. "And here!" The tiny characters cheered. "Those might be our real homes!" said blue Cinderella.

illustration.description: Esme thinking with her finger on her chin while the tiny mixed-up fairy tale characters gather around her hand. Marcus and Aisha are pointing excitedly at other glowing books on the shelf - a green one and a purple one. The tiny characters look hopeful, with their big expressive eyes showing relief and excitement.
illustration.alt: Esme and her friends helping the tiny mixed-up fairy tale characters find their correct story books

pages.text: They opened the green book first. "This feels like home!" said a tiny voice from inside. It was the real Little Red Riding Hood! She was the right color - not green. "I think I belong in the red book," she said. The green Little Red Riding Hood jumped up and down. "And I belong here! This is the Jolly Green Forest story!" The pink wolf looked confused. "But where do I belong?" he asked. Just then, Aisha opened the purple book. "Try this one!" she said.

illustration.description: Two versions of Little Red Riding Hood - one green and one normal colored - meeting each other as they emerge from different glowing books. The children watch as the characters figure out which book is their real home. The green version looks excited while pointing at the green book, and the normal version points toward the red book.
illustration.alt: Two Little Red Riding Hoods meeting and discovering their correct story books

pages.text: "Perfect!" squeaked a voice from the purple book. "I'm from the Pink Wolves of Wonder story!" The pink wolf wagged his tail. "That's me! That's my home!" One by one, they helped each character find their right story. Purple Goldilocks belonged in the Purple Bear Cave book. Blue Cinderella lived in the Ocean Princess story. "Thank you!" they all called as they jumped back into their correct books. "You saved us!" The books stopped glowing and looked normal again.

illustration.description: A joyful scene showing all the tiny fairy tale characters finding their correct books and jumping back inside. Each character is happily leaping toward their proper story book while waving goodbye to the children. Sparkles and magical light swirl around as they return home. Esme, Marcus, and Aisha wave back with big smiles.
illustration.alt: The fairy tale characters happily returning to their correct story books

pages.text: "Did that really happen?" whispered Marcus. Esme smiled and opened the red book again. Inside was a tiny note that said, "Thank you, Esme, Marcus, and Aisha! From your fairy tale friends." Aisha clapped her hands quietly. "It was real!" she said. Mrs. Chen walked over to them. "Did you find good books?" she asked. "The best books ever," said Esme. "They were full of surprises!" Mrs. Chen smiled. "The library is a magical place," she said.

illustration.description: The three children huddled together looking at a tiny thank-you note inside the red book. Their faces show wonder and happiness. Mrs. Chen approaches with a knowing smile. The library around them looks warm and inviting, with soft sunlight streaming through windows, suggesting the magic was real but now peaceful.
illustration.alt: Children finding a thank-you note from the fairy tale characters they helped

pages.text: From that day on, Esme, Marcus, and Aisha were extra careful with the library books. They always made sure to put them back in the right places. Sometimes, when they walked past the fairy tale section, they thought they saw a tiny sparkle. "Do you think they're happy in their stories?" asked Aisha one day. "I think so," said Esme. "And if they ever get mixed up again, we'll help them!" Marcus grinned. "We're the library helpers!" They felt very proud and special.

illustration.description: The three friends carefully placing books back on the library shelves, being extra gentle and thoughtful. They're smiling with pride and responsibility. A subtle magical sparkle can be seen coming from the fairy tale section. The illustration shows them as confident library helpers, standing tall with books in their hands.
illustration.alt: Esme, Marcus, and Aisha proudly taking care of library books

pages.text: Every library day became an adventure. Even when the books didn't glow, Esme and her friends found wonderful stories inside them. They read about dragons and castles, space ships and dinosaurs. But they never forgot their first magical day. "Books really are magic," Esme told her mom that night. "They take you to amazing places and sometimes, the characters need your help!" Her mom hugged her. "That's what makes reading so special," she said. Esme couldn't wait for library day again.

illustration.description: Esme at home in the evening, sitting on her bed and telling her mom (a woman with kind eyes and gentle expression) about her magical library adventure. Through the window, stars twinkle in the night sky. The room is cozy with warm light, and Esme gestures excitedly while her mom listens with love and wonder.
illustration.alt: Esme sharing her magical library story with her mom at bedtime

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What was wrong with Little Red Riding Hood when she first came out of the book?
choices: She was green instead of normal colored
questions.explanation: Little Red Riding Hood was green when she came out, but she was supposed to be normal colored because she was in the wrong story!

questions.question: How many friends helped Esme solve the fairy tale problem?
questions.explanation: Marcus and Aisha helped Esme, so that's two friends who worked together with her.

questions.question: What did the fairy tale characters leave for the children?
questions.explanation: The fairy tale characters left a tiny thank-you note in the red book to show they were grateful for the help.

questions.question: What color was the wolf supposed to be?
questions.explanation: The wolf said he was supposed to be gray, but he came out pink because he was in the wrong story.

questions.question: What did Esme and her friends become after helping the fairy tale characters?
questions.explanation: Marcus said 'We're the library helpers!' and they all felt proud to take special care of the books after their adventure.

pages.title: Help the Characters Find Their Colors!
pages.text: The fairy tale characters got mixed up! Help them by filling in the blanks with the RIGHT colors they should be.

blanks.sentence: Little Red Riding Hood should be _____ colored, not green.
The wolf should be _____, not pink.
Cinderella was _____ instead of her normal color.

pages.title: Library Adventure Word Hunt
pages.text: Complete the story about Esme's magical day by choosing the right words!

blanks.sentence: The _____ were glowing on the shelf.
Esme and her _____ helped the fairy tale characters.
The characters left a tiny _____ to say thank you.
